prompt: Render a 3D surface plot of the height grid [[0,1,2],[1,3,1],[2,1,0]], viewed at an angle, colored by height (low blue, high red), lit from the upper-left.
Draw a 3D contour/heightmap surface of height(x,y)=exp(-((x-0.5)^2+(y-0.5)^2)*8.0) seen in perspective, with thin contour lines at 6 levels overlaid.
Given the 4x4 grid of heights [[1,2,2,1],[2,4,3,2],[2,3,4,2],[1,2,2,1]], bilinearly interpolate it and render a smooth 3D surface lit from above.
Draw a 2D heatmap of the 5x5 grid where cell (i,j)=sin(i*1.2)*cos(j*1.2), using a blue->white->red diverging colormap with a thin border per cell.
Render a heatmap of f(x,y)=fract(sin(dot(floor(vec2(x,y)*8.0), vec2(12.9898,78.233)))*43758.5453) as an 8x8 grid of colored cells.
Draw a bar chart of the values [3,7,2,8,5,6,4], cyan bars on a dark background, evenly spaced, with a horizontal baseline axis.
Plot the line graph of the sequence [0.1,0.4,0.35,0.8,0.65,0.9,0.5,0.7] across the width as a connected polyline in yellow, with the area under it faintly filled.
Scatter-plot the points (0.2,0.3),(0.5,0.7),(0.8,0.4),(0.3,0.9),(0.65,0.2) as filled white circles on a grid background.
Draw a pie chart with slices of fraction 0.4, 0.25, 0.2, 0.15 in four distinct colors, starting from the top and going clockwise.
Draw the contour lines (isolines) of the scalar field f(x,y)=sin(5.0*x)*cos(5.0*y) at 8 evenly spaced levels, white lines on a dark field-colored background.
Render a topographic map of elevation = length(uv-0.5): hypsometric tint (green low, yellow/brown mid, white peak) with thin brown contour lines every 0.08.
Draw filled contour bands (8 discrete color steps) of f(x,y)=sin(8x)+cos(8y), like a weather pressure map.
Visualize the 2D vector field F(x,y)=(-y+0.5, x-0.5) as a regular grid of small arrows whose length and direction follow the field.
Domain-color the complex function f(z)=z*z-1: hue from the argument of f, brightness from its magnitude, over the square [-2,2]x[-2,2].
Draw streamlines of the flow field (sin(3.0*y), cos(3.0*x)) as flowing curved lines.
Plot the function y=0.5+0.4*sin(10.0/(x+0.1)) across the screen as a 2px white curve on black, with faint axes.
Compute and draw a Voronoi diagram for the seed points (0.2,0.2),(0.8,0.3),(0.5,0.8),(0.15,0.85),(0.7,0.6); fill each cell a distinct flat color and draw the cell borders in black.
Bilinearly interpolate the four corner colors red (bottom-left), green (bottom-right), blue (top-left), yellow (top-right) across the screen.
Render a 4x4 Bayer ordered-dithering of a left-to-right brightness gradient, producing a black-and-white dithered ramp.
Draw an isometric grid of 6 cubes with heights from the array [1,3,2,4,2,1], each cube shaded with 3 face brightnesses for a 2.5D look.
Draw an analog clock face: 12 hour ticks around a circle, plus hour, minute, and second hands rotating from iTime (treat iTime seconds as the time).
Render a circular progress gauge filled to 73 percent: a thick arc that starts at the top and sweeps clockwise, with the remaining arc dim.
Draw graph paper: minor grid lines every 0.05 and major grid lines every 0.2, with the x and y axes (through the center) highlighted in a brighter color.
Render a horizontal colormap legend bar going from blue (cold) on the left to red (hot) on the right, with 5 tick marks and a black border.
Ray-march and render a single cube rotating with iTime, with simple diffuse lighting from one direction on a dark background.
Draw a rotating 3D wireframe cube (12 edges) projected to 2D, white lines on black, spinning with iTime.
Render a sphere with latitude/longitude grid lines (a wireframe globe) rotating with iTime.
Draw a histogram of 8 bins with counts [2,5,9,14,11,7,3,1] as vertical bars, tallest normalized to full height, in a warm gradient.
Render marching-squares contour segments at level 0.5 of the field f(x,y)=0.5+0.5*sin(6x)*sin(6y), drawing only the iso-line.
Draw a simple node-link graph: 5 nodes at fixed positions connected by edges (1-2,2-3,3-4,4-5,5-1,1-3), nodes as circles, edges as lines.
Visualize a 1D array [0.2,0.9,0.5,0.7,0.3,0.8] as a horizontal stacked heat-strip, each segment colored by value on a viridis-like ramp.
Render a polar plot of r=0.5+0.3*cos(5.0*theta) (a 5-petal rose curve) as a bright line on a polar grid.

Draw a candlestick chart for 5 days with open/high/low/close = (0.3,0.6,0.2,0.5),(0.5,0.7,0.4,0.45),(0.45,0.5,0.3,0.35),(0.35,0.8,0.3,0.75),(0.75,0.9,0.6,0.85): green up days, red down days, wicks for high/low.
Render a 3D bar chart (city of blocks) from the 3x3 grid [[1,2,3],[2,4,1],[3,1,2]] in isometric projection, each bar height from its value.
Draw a confusion-matrix-style 3x3 grid with cell values [[0.9,0.05,0.05],[0.1,0.8,0.1],[0.0,0.2,0.8]], each cell shaded by value (dark=high) with the implied number drawn as brightness.
Visualize the gradient (slope direction) of the terrain h(x,y)=sin(4x)+sin(4y) as a hue-encoded direction field (hue = gradient angle).
